test.name: Project status request should not trigger proof-conclusion
test.description: User asks 'where are we?' — that's project-status. proof-conclusion writes a GPS-conformant proof narrative as a FamilySearch-uploadable document, not a project overview. The two skills both summarize a project but their audiences and formats differ.

input.user_message: Where are we with this project? Quick rundown of what's been done and what's pending.

negative.explanation: The user wants a status overview — questions, plans, what's done, what's pending — not the formal GPS proof narrative. project-status delivers the overview; proof-conclusion produces the proved/probable/possible/not-proved conclusion as a standalone document suitable for FamilySearch upload. Running proof-conclusion here would produce a much longer, formally-cited document the user didn't ask for.

judge_context: Should route to project-status rather than producing a full GPS proof narrative for a status check-in
